behavior_in_task_description.reason: instruction.md names the target file, the helper function name pattern 'readReactElementTypeof', the 'in' operator check, 'hasOwnProperty.call', and the two functions to modify.

no_solution_leakage.reason: Instruction explicitly dictates the helper name ('readReactElementTypeof'), the exact operators to use ('in' operator, 'hasOwnProperty.call'), and which functions to modify — leaving almost no design reasoning to the agent.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert on exact strings like "'$$typeof' in value", "hasOwnProperty.call(value, '$$typeof')", and the specific helper name 'readReactElementTypeof'; alternative correct fixes (e.g., try/catch wrapper, different helper name, Reflect.has) would fail.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Although many tests are string checks, the suite also runs yarn test, yarn flow, yarn lint, and license checks as subprocesses that execute code.

test_not_tautological.reason: f2p tests require specific structural changes (helper defined, called in both functions, old accesses removed) that a stub cannot satisfy.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All requirements in instruction.md (helper name, 'in' operator, hasOwnProperty.call, defined-before-use, replacing both call sites) are covered by tests.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are grouped with comment headers (Gates, Repo CI/CD, Fail-to-pass, Pass-to-pass) and each has a docstring.

structured_data_schema.reason: Task is a source code fix, not structured data output.

no_typos.reason: Paths and identifiers in instruction and tests match (packages/shared/ReactPerformanceTrackProperties.js, readReactElementTypeof).

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: instruction.md specifies the exact file 'packages/shared/ReactPerformanceTrackProperties.js', the exact helper name pattern, and the exact operators/methods to use, effectively dictating the implementation.
